client_information.landlord: Parkside Community Housing, Attn: Karen Morales

case_manager_information.case_manager_agency: Hennepin County Human Services – Disability Services Division

adaptations_requested.describe_adaptations_to_explore_line_1: Client sustained a T10 incomplete spinal cord injury in 2022 and uses a manual wheelchair for nearly all mobility. The primary goals of home environmental adaptations are to (1) enable safe, independent entry and exit from his second-floor apartment, (2) improve bathroom accessibility for transfers and hygiene, and (3) reduce fall risk in the kitchen and bedroom. Specific adaptations to explore include: an exterior modular ramp or platform lift from the parking lot up to the main building entrance (currently only stairs), automatic door opener or lever-handle hardware for the main building door and unit door, widening of the apartment entry door and bathroom door to a minimum clear opening of 36 inches, and installation of compliant threshold ramps or bevels at transitions that currently catch his wheelchair casters. The occupational therapist has noted that Michael frequently has to perform unsafe wheelies to clear thresholds and relies on others to hold heavy doors open, which has already resulted in one documented fall last winter when the door closed on his chair while he attempted to maneuver through snow and ice.
adaptations_requested.describe_adaptations_to_explore_line_2: Within the bathroom, client reports he is currently performing lateral transfers from his wheelchair to a standard-height toilet and into a bathtub using a small, non-secured shower chair. This setup is not safe and has led to near-falls, especially when his spasms increase. Adaptations to explore include: replacing the standard tub with a roll-in, curbless shower with non-slip flooring; installing a height-appropriate, wall-mounted fold-down shower bench; adding strategically placed grab bars (vertical and horizontal) near the toilet and in the shower area based on OT measurements; replacing the standard toilet with a comfort-height ADA-compliant model; and adjusting sink height and knee clearance so he can roll under in his wheelchair. In the kitchen, we would like to explore lowering a section of countertop for seated meal prep, relocating the microwave to a reachable height, and ensuring at least one bank of cabinets and drawers is accessible without overhead reaching that strains his shoulders. In the bedroom, we are interested in evaluating space for safe wheelchair turning radius, possible placement of a ceiling or portable overhead lift for bed transfers in the future, and reconfiguring closet rods and shelving so that he can independently access clothing without standing or using precarious reach techniques. We would also like recommendations for appropriate lighting controls (e.g., rocker switches, reachable heights) and potentially smart-home adaptations such as voice-controlled lights or thermostat to limit his need to transfer unnecessarily at night when his balance is worse.

waiver_information.diagnosis_codes: S24.103A – Unspecified injury of T10 level of thoracic spinal cord, incomplete; G82.20 – Paraplegia, unspecified; M62.81 – Muscle weakness (generalized); F32.9 – Major depressive disorder, single episode, unspecified (related to adjustment after injury)

billing_information.billing_agency: Hennepin County Human Services – Fiscal/Billing Unit
